description: DurableOutbox at-least-once across a crash/reconnect (protocol.md § Reliable Sync, #durable-outbox). Every frame is durably appended BEFORE it is sent, retained until the peer proves receipt (OutboxAck), and replayed from the peer's cursor on reconnect. A send that fails (or a process death between append and confirmed send) leaves the frame in the outbox; replay_from(cursor) re-sends every retained frame with epoch > cursor in order. Combined with the receiver's idempotent Ignore of already-applied deltas, replay delivers every op at least once and idempotent apply makes the net effect exactly-once (proven ReliableSync.outbox_at_least_once_exactly_once_effect). ack_through(e) is a retention signal only (safe to prune <= e), never a domain delivery authority.

scenarios.name: crash_between_append_and_ack_replays_on_reconnect
scenarios.description: Sender appends+sends deltas at epochs 41,42,43. The peer acks through 41 (OutboxAck{through_epoch:41}); the outbox may prune <= 41. The sender crashes and restarts; 42 and 43 are still durably in the outbox (append-before-send). On reconnect the peer advertises its cursor (last applied = 41); replay_from(41) re-sends 42 then 43 in order. The receiver applies 42 (base_epoch 41 == last_epoch) then 43. Exactly-once effect: no op lost (42,43 delivered), none doubled (41 already pruned/Ignored).

scenarios.name: send_failure_retains_frame_for_next_tick
scenarios.description: A send error does not unwind the driver and does not lose the frame: append succeeded, the send failed, so the frame stays in the outbox and is retried from the outbox on a later tick. (Contrast the pre-outbox bug: the out-epoch was bumped before the send, so a failed send left a permanent gap.)
